questions.question: What is the difference between a list and a tuple in Python?
options: B) Lists are mutable (can change); tuples are immutable (cannot change after creation)
D) Lists are ordered; tuples are not
questions.correctAnswer: B) Lists are mutable (can change); tuples are immutable (cannot change after creation)

questions.question: What does *args do in Python functions?
options: B) Collects extra positional arguments into a tuple
questions.correctAnswer: B) Collects extra positional arguments into a tuple

questions.question: What is a list comprehension?
options: B) Concise way to create list: [expr for item in iterable if condition]
questions.correctAnswer: B) Concise way to create list: [expr for item in iterable if condition]

questions.question: What is the difference between is and == in Python?
options: B) is checks identity (same object in memory); == checks equality (same value)
D) is is for strings only
questions.correctAnswer: B) is checks identity (same object in memory); == checks equality (same value)

questions.question: What is a Python decorator?
options: B) Function that wraps another function to add behavior — using @decorator syntax
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function that wraps another function to add behavior — using @decorator syntax

questions.question: What is a generator in Python?
options: B) Function using yield to return values lazily one at a time — memory efficient for large sequences
questions.correctAnswer: B) Function using yield to return values lazily one at a time — memory efficient for large sequences

questions.question: What is __init__ in Python classes?
options: B) Constructor method called when creating new instance — initializes object attributes
questions.correctAnswer: B) Constructor method called when creating new instance — initializes object attributes

questions.question: What is the difference between @staticmethod and @classmethod?
options: B) staticmethod has no access to class/instance; classmethod receives cls as first arg
questions.correctAnswer: B) staticmethod has no access to class/instance; classmethod receives cls as first arg

questions.question: What are Python dunder methods?
options: B) Special methods with double underscores (__str__, __len__) enabling operator overloading and built-in integration
questions.correctAnswer: B) Special methods with double underscores (__str__, __len__) enabling operator overloading and built-in integration

questions.question: What is a Python context manager (with statement)?
options: B) Protocol using __enter__/__exit__ ensuring resource cleanup — automatic close/release even on exception
questions.correctAnswer: B) Protocol using __enter__/__exit__ ensuring resource cleanup — automatic close/release even on exception

questions.question: What is duck typing in Python?
options: B) If object has required methods/attributes, it works — 'if it walks like a duck and quacks like a duck'
questions.correctAnswer: B) If object has required methods/attributes, it works — 'if it walks like a duck and quacks like a duck'

questions.question: What is the GIL (Global Interpreter Lock)?
options: B) Mutex preventing multiple threads from executing Python bytecode simultaneously in CPython
questions.correctAnswer: B) Mutex preventing multiple threads from executing Python bytecode simultaneously in CPython

questions.question: What is the difference between deep copy and shallow copy?
options: B) Shallow copy copies object but shares references to nested objects; deep copy recursively copies everything
C) Deep copy is slower but same result
D) Shallow copy fails for nested objects
questions.correctAnswer: B) Shallow copy copies object but shares references to nested objects; deep copy recursively copies everything

questions.question: What is a lambda function?
options: B) Anonymous single-expression function: lambda args: expression
questions.correctAnswer: B) Anonymous single-expression function: lambda args: expression

questions.question: What is *args vs **kwargs?
options: B) *args collects positional args as tuple; **kwargs collects keyword args as dict
questions.correctAnswer: B) *args collects positional args as tuple; **kwargs collects keyword args as dict

questions.question: What is Python's MRO (Method Resolution Order)?
options: B) Order Python searches for method in class hierarchy — uses C3 linearization algorithm
questions.correctAnswer: B) Order Python searches for method in class hierarchy — uses C3 linearization algorithm

questions.question: What is a Python dataclass?
options: B) @dataclass decorator auto-generates __init__, __repr__, __eq__ from class variable annotations
questions.correctAnswer: B) @dataclass decorator auto-generates __init__, __repr__, __eq__ from class variable annotations

questions.question: What is asyncio in Python?
options: B) Standard library for writing concurrent code using async/await — single-threaded cooperative multitasking
questions.correctAnswer: B) Standard library for writing concurrent code using async/await — single-threaded cooperative multitasking

questions.question: What is the difference between a set and a frozenset?
options: B) set is mutable (add/remove elements); frozenset is immutable and can be used as dict key
questions.correctAnswer: B) set is mutable (add/remove elements); frozenset is immutable and can be used as dict key

questions.question: What is Python's __slots__?
options: B) Class variable restricting instance attributes to listed names — reduces memory by avoiding __dict__
questions.correctAnswer: B) Class variable restricting instance attributes to listed names — reduces memory by avoiding __dict__

questions.question: Which Python datatype is immutable?
questions.correctAnswer: D) Tuple

questions.question: Which Python keyword defines function?
questions.correctAnswer: B) def
